message:
feat(tui): expand TUI rendering and sync beads issue tracker history

- src/tui.rs: +326 lines — expand TUI with improved rendering,
  additional screen layouts, and enhanced issue display capabilities
- Sync beads issue tracker state: update issues.jsonl, add current
  .br_history snapshots, remove stale early-morning snapshots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -150,7 +150,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-7oo.4.2","title":"Port board-mode inline expansion and detail-scrolling parity","description":"Background:
Rust board mode is already functional, but the audit still found missing legacy behaviors such as inline expansion and richer detail scrolling or navigation semantics.

Goal:
Upgrade board mode from “usable” to legacy-equivalent for serious triage and planning workflows.","design":"Implementation expectations: Port the board interactions that materially change how users inspect and manipulate lane content. Preserve the recent Rust board navigation work instead of replacing it wholesale. Ensure inline expansion, detail scrolling, and board navigation preserve orientation so users do not lose lane, selection, or expanded-state context. Extend keyflow and snapshot coverage to reflect the richer board-state model.","acceptance_criteria":"Board mode supports the agreed inline and detail interactions needed for parity. Users can expand, inspect, and scroll board content without losing track of their current lane or selection context. Keyflow tests exercise the richer board interactions without relying on manual testing, and the board partial-parity row can be reevaluated from evidence after this bead.","notes":"This bead is serialized after the main-detail bead because both heavily touch shared TUI detail rendering and selection behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:53.348634854Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T00:35:34.512220296Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T00:35:34.510131184Z","close_reason":"Added board detail scrolling state/rendering parity, wired Ctrl+j/k/u/d detail scrolling with selection resets, and added focused board-detail scroll coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","legacy-port","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:53.348634854Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:56.220594489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-7oo.4.3","title":"Port insights heatmap and drill-down interactions to parity","description":"Background:
Rust insights mode has solid core metrics and toggles, but the legacy Go UI still exposes richer exploratory affordances such as heatmap or drill-down style interactions.

Goal:
Make insights mode comparable to the legacy investigative workflow instead of merely presenting summary metrics.","design":"Implementation expectations: Identify the specific interaction and visualization semantics that made the Go insights view more powerful. Port those capabilities in a Rust-native way that remains testable and coherent with the existing view-mode architecture. Keep explanation and calculation-proof affordances intact while expanding exploratory depth, and preserve clear context about what metric, slice, or drill-down target the user is currently inspecting.","acceptance_criteria":"Insights mode supports the agreed parity interactions and richer drill-down behavior. Users can tell what they are drilling into, navigate back out without losing context, and understand the active metric or focus area. Snapshot or keyflow coverage demonstrates the new interactions, and the resulting view is meaningfully closer to the legacy power-user workflow.","notes":"This bead follows board work for merge-safety reasons: both are high-churn edits in the same TUI module and test set.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:53.539424675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:00:40.075759754Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T01:00:40.073536541Z","close_reason":"Added an insights heatmap submode with score-depth buckets, Enter/Esc drill behavior, list-pane navigation, detail-context sync, focused tests, and updated insights snapshots.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["insights","legacy-port","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:53.539424675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:56.313572641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-7oo.4.4","title":"Port the dedicated visual graph pane to parity","description":"Background:
Rust graph mode already exposes useful graph data, but the audit still found a gap versus the dedicated legacy visual graph pane and its richer presentation model.

Goal:
Close the remaining graph-view fidelity gap so graph mode supports the same style of interactive dependency investigation as legacy Go.","design":"Implementation expectations: Port the missing visual and interaction semantics that made the legacy graph pane distinct. Preserve the stronger current Rust metrics and deterministic rendering qualities while improving fidelity. Keep the result snapshot-friendly and robust under different terminal widths, sparse graphs, and focused-node exploration, and make focused node or edge context explicit enough that the user always knows what part of the graph they are inspecting.","acceptance_criteria":"Graph mode reaches the agreed parity target for presentation and interaction semantics. It remains legible across representative terminal widths and sparse or dense graph states, and it keeps the currently focused node or edge context obvious. Snapshot, debug-render, or keyflow coverage demonstrates the richer graph experience, and the graph partial-parity row can be revisited from concrete evidence after this bead.","notes":"This bead intentionally comes after the earlier TUI tasks to serialize edits to the same module and reduce the chance of long-lived merge conflicts.

Progress 2026-03-09:
- consulted legacy graph pane implementation in /dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/graph.go and current Rust graph mode in src/tui.rs
- added shared graph-visible sorting helper so list/detail use the same deterministic node ordering
- graph detail pane now surfaces explicit focus context for the selected node and currently focused dependency edge
- added focused TUI regression test for node/edge focus context

Verified:
- rch cargo test --lib graph_detail_text_surfaces_focus_context_for_node_and_edge
- rch cargo check --all-targets

Remaining in this bead:
- richer visual graph-pane fidelity beyond focus-context text, likely in the detail rendering/layout and follow-on snapshots/keyflows

Progress 2026-03-09 (continued):
- consulted legacy /dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/graph.go again and ported the next parity slice into src/tui.rs
- graph detail now uses legacy-style dependency headers plus richer related-node labels with titles/statuses
- narrow graph breakpoint now renders as a single visual graph pane titled Graph View, matching legacy compact behavior more closely
- graph footer copy now surfaces the actual graph navigation/search/open-detail affordances
- refreshed graph snapshots to encode the new narrow/medium/wide render outputs

Verified:
- rch cargo test --lib graph_
- rch cargo test --lib history_git_search_zero_results_show_explicit_message
- rch cargo check --all-targets

Residual:
- repo still has pre-existing warnings in src/tui.rs and tests/export_pages.rs; no new graph-slice failures remain
- remaining graph-parity work, if any, is beyond this slice and should focus on deeper visual box/connector fidelity or follow-on graph keyflows rather than the now-fixed context/layout gap","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:53.761120363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:54.119268915Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:54.118701702Z","close_reason":"Completed visual graph parity pass with render, focus, and snapshot coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["graph","legacy-port","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:53.761120363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:56.407282865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":101,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.4","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09:
- consulted corrected legacy source /dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/graph.go for the next parity slice
- ported legacy narrow-width graph behavior so Graph mode collapses to a single detail pane on narrow terminals while keeping node/edge focus context explicit
- kept medium/wide two-pane graph layout and refreshed graph snapshots to match the richer focus-context/detail rendering
- added a focused regression test for narrow graph layout in src/tui.rs

Verified via rch:
- cargo test --lib graph_mode_narrow_uses_single_detail_pane_layout
- cargo test --lib graph_detail_text_surfaces_focus_context_for_node_and_edge
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo test --lib snap_graph_

Remaining in this bead:
- higher-fidelity graph presentation beyond the current snapshot-friendly ASCII/layout parity slice if we want to chase more of the legacy pane polish.","created_at":"2026-03-09T18:56:58Z"},{"id":102,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.4","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (visual graph parity pass):
- consulted /dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/graph.go again and ported the legacy-style visual graph composition deeper into src/tui.rs
- graph detail now renders width-aware centered ego boxes, relationship node boxes, and fan connectors instead of only the earlier vertical relationship report
- preserved Rust-only focus semantics (focused node/edge context text and edge cursor behavior) while making the visual pane closer to legacy Go
- refreshed graph snapshots to the new visual layout and updated them to insta's current metadata format

Verified via rch:
- cargo test --lib graph_detail_text_uses_legacy_relationship_headers_and_legend
- cargo test --lib graph_detail_text_renders_visual_graph_content
- cargo test --lib graph_detail_text_surfaces_focus_context_for_node_and_edge
- cargo test --lib snap_graph_
- cargo check --all-targets

Notes:
- all checks passed for this slice
- remaining warnings are pre-existing: unreachable KeyCode::Char('a') in src/tui.rs and unrelated warnings in tests/export_pages.rs
- if more graph parity is needed after this, it is likely in follow-on keyflows/paging or other TUI surfaces rather than the core visual graph composition itself.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-7oo.4.5","title":"Expand TUI snapshots, state tests, and keyflows for the remaining parity surfaces","description":"Background:
The TUI already has strong snapshot and keyflow infrastructure, which makes it possible to encode parity precisely if we use it aggressively enough before we add full end-to-end journey coverage.

Goal:
Turn the remaining TUI parity claims into tested, reviewable render, state, and interaction contracts.","design":"Implementation expectations: Add or refresh snapshots, debug-render fixtures, deterministic state tests, and keyflow journeys for the main, board, insights, and graph deltas closed in this track. Cover representative happy-path renders plus edge and failure-oriented states such as narrow terminals, long content, empty datasets, and focus or selection edge cases. Keep tests deterministic and easy to interpret from failures. Use this bead as the code-level closure gate for all remaining TUI parity rows, and shape its outputs so the later end-to-end TUI journey bead can produce richer logs without redefining the contract.","acceptance_criteria":"New TUI parity behavior is covered by snapshots, deterministic state tests, or keyflows rather than just manual observation. The suite spans happy-path, representative edge-case, and failure-oriented render or interaction states, and failures clearly indicate which view, geometry, or interaction regressed. Final parity docs can cite this bead as evidence for TUI completion or residual gaps, and the later e2e bead has a stable contract to exercise.","notes":"This is the TUI track’s code-level closure bead and remains blocked on the preceding serialized implementation tasks. End-to-end terminal journeys and logging expectations are handled by its companion e2e bead.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeBear","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:53.947641776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T01:14:46.572366891Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["legacy-port","parity","tests","tui","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:53.947641776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:56.497063626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:56.592070510Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:56.694111402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:56.788726862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":103,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Start 2026-03-09:
- claimed immediately after closing bd-7oo.4.4 because the graph-visual parity pass unblocked this verification bead
- current verified inputs for this bead now include: graph focus-context tests, narrow graph layout regression, refreshed graph snapshots, and the new width-aware visual graph rendering contract
- next work should concentrate on converting remaining TUI parity claims into deterministic snapshots, state tests, and keyflows across main/board/insights/graph edge cases rather than more manual UI comparison","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:17:10Z"},{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09:
- fixed the Rust TUI `a` key conflict so `a` now clears an active open/closed/ready filter first, then toggles Actionable mode once the list is back at `all`; this restores Actionable reachability without regressing the existing quick-filter clear path
- ported the Actionable pane closer to legacy `/dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/actionable.go` by adding the ACTIONABLE ITEMS summary header, recommended-start callout, track-first list structure, and richer track detail text with claim/unblock context
- added deterministic coverage for actionable toggle/return flow, filter-clear precedence, legacy-shaped actionable text, and refreshed the `snap_actionable_detail_focus` snapshot
- verification via `rch`: `cargo test --lib actionable`, `cargo test --lib keyflow_full_mode_tour`, and `cargo check --all-targets` all passed; residual warnings remain unrelated in `tests/export_pages.rs`","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:16:41Z"},{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (history search follow-up):
- fixed git-mode history match cycling so `n/N` now walks the same filtered commit set that the visible history list uses, instead of the raw commit cache; this means file-tree filters and confidence thresholds now constrain search matches consistently
- added explicit git-mode history search tests for commit-message, SHA-prefix, and author matching, plus a regression test proving file-tree filters narrow the search-match set
- verification via `rch`: `cargo test --lib history_git_search` passed","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:20:33Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (help overlay follow-up):
- updated the help overlay so the Views section now advertises `a` for Actionable mode, matching the restored keypath and the legacy help matrix, and removed the misleading `a = show all` filter-only wording
- tightened compact/wide help tests so Actionable stays visible in the rendered help contract
- verification via `rch`: `cargo test --lib help_overlay_text_` passed","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:29:43Z"},{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (graph overflow parity):
- added deterministic graph detail tests covering the legacy /dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/graph_test.go many-blockers and many-dependents cases, asserting the Rust graph pane shows the first five related nodes plus a +N more overflow summary instead of silently dropping excess fan-in/fan-out context
- the only code change needed in src/tui.rs after adding coverage was to make the new tests select the anchor node by ID (MAIN/ROOT) rather than assuming analyzer order; the renderer behavior itself already matched the expected legacy overflow contract once exercised correctly
- while re-opening lib-test verification, fixed an unrelated existing compile blocker in src/analysis/graph.rs by restoring the missing triangle_issues() helper used by the two-phase metric tests
- verification via rch: cargo test --lib graph_detail_text_many_ passed, and cargo check --all-targets passed after moving CARGO_TARGET_DIR onto the project volume to avoid remote /tmp exhaustion; only the pre-existing warnings in tests/export_pages.rs remain
- prioritized next parity gaps from the scout pass: (1) history still lacks the legacy multi-pane standard/wide workflow, (2) board is still summary-first rather than the adaptive kanban-first layout, (3) graph still differs architecturally from the legacy whole-graph visualizer, and (4) insights still lacks the legacy Keystones/Priority panels","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:42:11Z"},{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (insights panel parity):
- restored two legacy-style insights surfaces in src/tui.rs without changing public analysis APIs: , derived directly from existing  + unblock counts, and , derived from the existing Analyzer::priority() recommendation path
- updated the insights panel enum, labels, and wrap order so panel cycling now includes Bottlenecks -> Keystones -> Critical Path -> Influencers -> Betweenness -> Hubs -> Authorities -> K-Core -> Cut Points -> Slack -> Cycles -> Priority -> Bottlenecks
- added deterministic tests for panel cycling and for the legacy-style keystone/priority row shapes, and refreshed the  snapshot to lock the new panel order
- verification via rch: 
running 23 tests
test analysis::tests::insights_promote_primary_blocker_for_bd_3q0_slice ... ok
test analysis::tests::fast_insights_still_works ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_detail_shows_deps_with_cursor_and_j_k_works ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_detail_shows_all_metrics_for_focused_issue ... ok
test analysis::tests::fast_then_slow_produces_same_insights_as_full ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_heatmap_empty_grid_renders_without_panic ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_heatmap_drill_selection_syncs_detail_context ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_heatmap_list_keys_stay_in_list_focus ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_list_header_shows_search_hint ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_heatmap_toggle_and_escape_drill_preserve_mode ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_mode_e_and_x_toggle_explanations_and_calc_proof ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_keystones_and_priority_panels_render_legacy_style_rows ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_mode_search_query_and_match_cycling_work ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_mode_renders_rank_sections ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_mode_h_l_switch_focus_panes ... ok
test tui::tests::keyflow_main_to_insights_explore_return ... ok
test tui::tests::insights_panel_s_cycles_through_all_panels ... ok
test tui::tests::snap_insights_narrow ... ok
test tui::tests::snap_insights_medium ... ok
test tui::tests::snap_after_insights_panel_cycle ... ok
test tui::tests::snap_insights_wide ... ok
test tui::tests::snap_insights_heatmap_drill ... ok
test export_pages::tests::insights_json_has_bottlenecks_key ... ok

test result: ok. 23 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 764 filtered out; finished in 0.43s passed and updated the snapshot;  also passed on the current tree with only the pre-existing warnings in tests/export_pages.rs","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:47:41Z"},{"id":109,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction 2026-03-09 (insights panel parity, clean record):
- restored two legacy-style insights surfaces in src/tui.rs without changing public analysis APIs: Keystones, derived from existing critical_depth plus unblock counts, and Priority, derived from the existing Analyzer::priority() recommendation path
- updated the insights panel enum, labels, and wrap order so panel cycling now includes Bottlenecks -> Keystones -> Critical Path -> Influencers -> Betweenness -> Hubs -> Authorities -> K-Core -> Cut Points -> Slack -> Cycles -> Priority -> Bottlenecks
- added deterministic tests for panel cycling and for the keystone/priority row shapes, and refreshed the snap_after_insights_panel_cycle snapshot to lock the new panel order
- verification via rch: INSTA_UPDATE=always cargo test --lib insights_ passed and updated the snapshot; cargo check --all-targets also passed on the current tree with only the pre-existing warnings in tests/export_pages.rs
- note: the immediately previous bead comment was shell-mangled by accidental backtick substitution; use this entry as the authoritative record for the insights slice","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:48:00Z"},{"id":110,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Start 2026-03-09 (history multi-pane parity slice):
- next spec-first porting target is the legacy history workflow from /dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/history.go and history_test.go because it is the highest-value remaining parity gap that is actionable without waiting on blocked beads
- immediate goal is to extract the exact standard/wide pane contract and encode it in Rust tests before changing src/tui.rs
- larger structural gaps still queued after this slice: board kanban-first layout, full legacy whole-graph visualizer behavior, and any remaining history polish beyond the multi-pane workflow","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:18:27Z"},{"id":111,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (history layout + mode-indicator parity):
- ported legacy `/dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/history.go` standard/wide history workflow into `src/tui.rs`: standard history now renders list/middle/detail, wide bead mode adds the timeline pane, and Tab/j/k navigation now includes the middle pane consistently for bead and git history
- refreshed deterministic coverage for history layout breakpoints, three-pane focus cycling, middle-pane navigation, and the standard/wide bead+git render contracts; refreshed the history snapshots to match the new legacy-shaped layouts
- added the legacy history mode indicator labels/icons to the top status line (`◈ Beads`, `◉ Git`) and covered them with focused tests plus updated history snapshots
- verification via `rch`: `cargo test --lib history_` passed (44 tests), and `cargo check --all-targets` passed after the icon slice; only the pre-existing warnings in `tests/export_pages.rs` remain","created_at":"2026-03-09T23:06:12Z"},{"id":112,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Ported the next history parity slice from /dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/history.go: when git-history compat events are available, the history detail pane now renders a legacy-style compact lifecycle section with event counts, reverse-chronological icons, relative times, initials, and +N overflow summary. Added helper coverage plus detail-pane assertions in src/tui.rs. Verified via rch: cargo test --lib history_ (53 passed) and cargo check --all-targets (passes; only pre-existing warnings remain in tests/export_pages.rs).","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:10:43Z"},{"id":113,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Follow-up parity increment: the wide history timeline pane now uses legacy summary cues when git-history compat data exists, including the compact lifecycle strip plus cycle and average-confidence summary lines. Added focused coverage in src/tui.rs and verified with rch: cargo test --lib history_timeline_text_uses_legacy_summary_when_git_history_exists.","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:14:46Z"}]}

- claimed immediately after closing bd-7oo.4.4 because the graph-visual parity pass unblocked this verification bead
- current verified inputs for this bead now include: graph focus-context tests, narrow graph layout regression, refreshed graph snapshots, and the new width-aware visual graph rendering contract
- next work should concentrate on converting remaining TUI parity claims into deterministic snapshots, state tests, and keyflows across main/board/insights/graph edge cases rather than more manual UI comparison","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:17:10Z"},{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09:
- fixed the Rust TUI `a` key conflict so `a` now clears an active open/closed/ready filter first, then toggles Actionable mode once the list is back at `all`; this restores Actionable reachability without regressing the existing quick-filter clear path
- ported the Actionable pane closer to legacy `/dp/beads_viewer/pkg/ui/actionable.go` by adding the ACTIONABLE ITEMS summary header, recommended-start callout, track-first list structure, and richer track detail text with claim/unblock context
- added deterministic coverage for actionable toggle/return flow, filter-clear precedence, legacy-shaped actionable text, and refreshed the `snap_actionable_detail_focus` snapshot
- verification via `rch`: `cargo test --lib actionable`, `cargo test --lib keyflow_full_mode_tour`, and `cargo check --all-targets` all passed; residual warnings remain unrelated in `tests/export_pages.rs`","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:16:41Z"},{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (history search follow-up):

- added explicit git-mode history search tests for commit-message, SHA-prefix, and author matching, plus a regression test proving file-tree filters narrow the search-match set
- verification via `rch`: `cargo test --lib history_git_search` passed","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:20:33Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (help overlay follow-up):

- the only code change needed in src/tui.rs after adding coverage was to make the new tests select the anchor node by ID (MAIN/ROOT) rather than assuming analyzer order; the renderer behavior itself already matched the expected legacy overflow contract once exercised correctly
- while re-opening lib-test verification, fixed an unrelated existing compile blocker in src/analysis/graph.rs by restoring the missing triangle_issues() helper used by the two-phase metric tests
- verification via rch: cargo test --lib graph_detail_text_many_ passed, and cargo check --all-targets passed after moving CARGO_TARGET_DIR onto the project volume to avoid remote /tmp exhaustion; only the pre-existing warnings in tests/export_pages.rs remain
- prioritized next parity gaps from the scout pass: (1) history still lacks the legacy multi-pane standard/wide workflow, (2) board is still summary-first rather than the adaptive kanban-first layout, (3) graph still differs architecturally from the legacy whole-graph visualizer, and (4) insights still lacks the legacy Keystones/Priority panels","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:42:11Z"},{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress 2026-03-09 (insights panel parity):

 {"id":"bd-7oo.4.6","title":"Add TUI parity end-to-end journeys with failure artifact capture","description":"Background:
The remaining TUI gaps are strongly interaction-driven, so snapshots alone are not enough to prove that the finished experience matches the intended legacy workflows.

Goal:
Add end-to-end TUI journeys with retained artifacts so regressions in main, board, insights, and graph workflows are easy to reproduce and understand.","design":"Implementation expectations: Create or extend a deterministic TUI journey runner, such as a scripted keystroke harness, VHS capture, or another existing terminal-test tool the repo already uses, and use it to exercise the main-to-board-to-insights-to-graph investigative flows. Capture the key sequence, terminal dimensions, screen dumps or recordings, environment or fixture summaries, and the path to any failing snapshot or diff artifact. Include both a primary success journey and at least one failure-oriented or edge scenario such as narrow geometry, empty data, or a deliberately broken fixture that proves the state-dump diagnostics are useful. Make the output legible enough that a future agent can tell which view transition or interaction broke without re-instrumenting the harness. Wire the runner into the main e2e entrypoint or a documented parity-runner so interactive proof is as discoverable as the rest of the parity suite.","acceptance_criteria":"- The TUI e2e journeys cover the core interactive workflows closed by this parity push and include at least one representative edge or failure-oriented run with retained artifacts.
- Failures preserve enough information to show which keyflow, terminal geometry, render state, or fixture caused the regression.
- The journey runner is reachable from a documented e2e entrypoint or parity-runner workflow.
- This bead is required evidence before docs or the final parity sweep can claim the TUI track is complete.","notes":"This bead is the heaviest TUI-proof layer and complements, rather than replaces, the faster snapshot and keyflow tests.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:49:04.812410360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T00:13:33.359089271Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","legacy-port","logging","parity","tests","tui","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:49:04.812410360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:53:50.571904659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-7oo.4.7","title":"Add TUI parity end-to-end journeys with verbose state-dump logging","description":"Background:
Snapshots and keyflows are necessary but not sufficient for the remaining TUI parity work. We also need an operator-level journey that records how the terminal experience behaves across the major parity surfaces and leaves usable diagnostics behind when it fails.

Goal:
Add end-to-end TUI parity journeys with verbose state-dump logging for the remaining main, board, insights, and graph workflows.","design":"Implementation expectations:
- Add or extend a recorder-driven or scripted terminal journey that exercises the remaining parity surfaces at stable terminal sizes and fixture states.
- Emit timestamped step banners, terminal dimensions, keypress traces or scripted actions, snapshot paths, and any state-dump or debug-render artifact locations.
- Preserve enough artifact information on failure that future agents can tell whether the regression lives in rendering, focus or selection state, or a view-specific interaction.
- Keep the journey deterministic enough to run under `rch` without interactive supervision.","acceptance_criteria":"- A deterministic end-to-end TUI journey validates the remaining parity surfaces under `rch`.
- Logs and captured artifacts make it obvious which view, interaction, or render state regressed.
- The journey is cited by the final verification bead and final parity docs as part of the required TUI evidence chain.","notes":"This bead is intentionally separate from the snapshot and keyflow bead so the plan distinguishes between code-level render contracts and operator-level terminal journeys with richer diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:54:27.016971895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T00:03:03.133586750Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T23:55:55.004238636Z","close_reason":"Superseded by bd-7oo.4.6; duplicate TUI e2e planning bead created during concurrent refinement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","legacy-port","logging","parity","tests","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:54:27.016971895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-7oo.5","title":"Align parity documentation and architecture promises with reality","description":"Background:
The audit showed that several parity docs were stale or overly optimistic, especially around hooks/workspace/history, history search modes, export/pages, and the claimed asupersync integration state.

Goal:
Make the documentation trustworthy so future work is driven by real implementation state instead of outdated claims or aspirational wording.","design":"Approach:
- Separate parity truth maintenance from implementation work, but block final closure on it.
- Treat `FEATURE_PARITY.md` as a living ledger backed by tests, not a marketing artifact.
- Clarify how asupersync fits into the architecture goal versus the narrower legacy-parity objective.
- Make the docs the discoverable control plane for verification: they should point to the unit, integration, and e2e entrypoints plus the logging and artifact conventions introduced by this parity push.
- Keep documentation updates trailing validated behavior so the written story always reflects the proven system rather than aspirational scope.","acceptance_criteria":"- Child tasks under this feature are completed.
- Parity and architecture docs match actual code/test state.
- Future agents can use the docs as trustworthy operational context instead of stale folklore.
- The docs tell a maintainer how to rerun the parity proof set and where to look when one of the parity scripts fails.","notes":"This feature intentionally comes later in the graph because docs should trail validated behavior, not run ahead of it.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:39:28.728487786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T23:59:55.350177032Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T23:59:55.349644745Z","close_reason":"Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md and README.md to reflect actual implementation state: TUI views promoted from partial→complete (Main/Board/Insights/Graph/keybindings), Export/Pages marked complete (wizard/preview/watch all implemented), history search modes (5 types) documented as complete, test counts updated (872 lib, 1248 total), 7 Rust-only view modes documented, proof-set rerun section added, parity summary corrected (125 complete, 1 excluded), known risks pruned. README test counts and TUI key map updated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","docs","legacy-port","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.5","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:39:28.728487786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
